title: Song of the Elves
wikitext:
{{External|rs=Plague's End}} {{Redirect3|Sote|For the desert quest, see [[Spirits of the Elid]]. For the boss in Theatre of Blood, see [[Sotetseg]]. For the music track, see [[Song of the Elves (music track)]]}} {{Has quick guide}} {{Infobox Quest |name = Song of the Elves |number = 143 |image = [[File:Song of the Elves.png]] |release = [[25 July]] [[2019]] |update = Song of the Elves |aka = SotE |members = Yes |series = [[Quests/Series#Elf|Elf]], #8 |developer = ''' Development and testing''': Mod Ed, Mod Curse, Mod Lenny, Mod Tide
'''Art and graphics''': Mod Gee, Mod Ghost, Mod West, Mod Ry
'''Audio''': Mod Ian, Mod Slippers, Mod Surma }} '''Song of the Elves''' is the eighth and final [[quest]] in the [[List of quest series#Elf series|Elf quest series]]. The quest was announced at [[RuneFest|RuneFest 2018]] and was released on [[25 July]] [[2019]]. Completion of the quest unlocks access to the elven city of [[Prifddinas]]. The quest follows up on the events of [[Mourning's End Part II]]. With the [[Temple of Light]] now sealed for another thousand years, the player joins the rebel elves in exposing the [[Biohazard|hoax of the plague]] and starting a revolution against [[Lord Iorwerth]] and [[King Lathas]]. ==Details== {{Quest details |start = Talk to [[Edmond]], who can be found at his home in East Ardougne, north of the [[Flying Horse Inn]]. |startmap = 2569,3333 |difficulty = Grandmaster |description = Our story begins with you, the hero, answering the call to adventure when Edmond, a resident of Ardougne, asks for your aid. By rescuing his daughter Elena from Plague City, the quarantined West Ardougne, you begin to unravel the mystery surrounding the disease. With her help, you discover the plague is a hoax - a plot by King Lathas to section off the West Ardougne. By confronting him, you find out this was done to protect the rest of Gielinor from the threat in the elven lands. The king sends you on a mission to travel through the Underground Pass and help his ally, Lord Iorwerth, in eradicating his brother King Tyras who threatens to revive the mysterious Dark Lord. After an arduous journey through the pass, you meet with Iorwerth and his forces to end Tyras' evil once and for all. Upon your return to Lathas you are intercepted by Arianwyn, a rebel elf who opposes Lord Iorwerth. He informs you of Lathas and Iorwerth's deception. They manipulated you into removing their final opposition and were now free to bring the Dark Lord back to Gielinor for their own intentions. You work with the elven rebellion to discover the purpose of the plague was to disguise the digsite of the long lost Temple of Light. An ancient place that is key to bringing back the Dark Lord. To make amends for your past mistakes and put a halt in King Lathas' and Lord Iorwerth's plans, you solve the intricate light puzzle designed to protect the Death Altar within the temple. After sealing off the temple and preventing the return of the Dark Lord, it's now time to turn your attention to King Lathas and Lord Iorwerth to end their schemes once and for all in Song of the Elves. |length = Very Long |requirements = *Completion of the following quests: **[[Mourning's End Part II]] ***[[Mourning's End Part I]] ****[[Big Chompy Bird Hunting]] ****[[Sheep Herder]] ****[[Roving Elves]] *****[[Waterfall Quest]] *****[[Regicide]] ******[[Underground Pass]] *******[[Biohazard]] ********[[Plague City]] **[[Making History]] ***[[Priest in Peril]] ***[[The Restless Ghost]] **[[Druidic Ritual]] *{{SCP|Agility|70|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}} *{{SCP|Construction|70|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}} *{{SCP|Farming|70|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}} *{{SCP|Herblore|70|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}} *{{SCP|Hunter|70|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}} *{{SCP|Mining|70|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}} *{{SCP|Smithing|70|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}} *{{SCP|Woodcutting|70|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}} |items = *[[Steel full helm]] *[[Steel platebody]] *[[Steel platelegs]] *[[Red dye]] *[[Purple dye]] *[[Mourner gear]] including [[gas mask]] ([[plague jacket]] and [[plague trousers|trousers]] do not work) *[[Silk]] *[[Runite bar]] *[[Limestone brick]] x 8 *[[Tinderbox]] *[[Rope]] *[[Nature rune]] *[[Flowers]] from [[mithril seeds]] or [[adamant seeds]], or an [[irit leaf]] *[[Adamant chainbody]] (consumed during the quest) *[[Wine of zamorak]] OR [[Zamorak brew|Zamorak brew(4)]] *[[Cabbage]] (obtainable during the quest) *[[Black knife]] OR [[black dagger]] *[[Cadantine seed]] *[[Seed dibber]] (or partial completion of the [[Farming]] portion of the [[Barbarian Training]] [[miniquest]]) *[[Vial of water]] *[[Pestle and mortar]] *[[Hammer]] *[[Saw]] *[[Pickaxe]] *[[Axe]] *[[Spade]] |kills = *21 [[Mourner (Song of the Elves)|Mourners]] (level 24, 51, and 106) *[[Paladin (Song of the Elves)|Paladin]] (level 49 and 118) *[[Knight of Ardougne (Song of the Elves)|Knight of Ardougne]] (level 27 and 53) *Several [[Iorwerth Archer]]s and [[Iorwerth Warrior|Warriors]] (level 90 and 108) *[[Arianwyn]] (level 212) *[[Essyllt]] (level 236) *[[Fragment of Seren]] (level 494) |recommended = *{{SCP|Combat|95|link=yes}} *{{SCP|Magic|80+|link=yes}} for the [[Fragment of Seren]]. *{{SCP|Prayer|40|link=yes}} to be able to use [[Protect from Missiles]] at boss fight. *Completion of [[Desert Treasure I]] for [[Ancient Magicks]]. *High healing food, such as [[monkfish]] or [[sharks]]. *[[West Ardougne Teleport]] *[[Games necklace]] for teleportation to [[Baxtorian Falls]]. *[[Weight-reducing clothing]] and energy restoration items such as [[stamina potion]]. *Charged [[teleport crystal]]s to skip large amounts of walking. *3x [[Iorwerth camp teleport]] or equivalent method. *[[Zul-Andra teleport]] |ironman = Obtaining a [[runite bar]]: *Exchange [[rune platelegs]] at the Aldarin windmill [[Nest (nice)|nest]] to obtain it. [[Rune platelegs]] can be purchased at [[Scavvo's Rune Store.]] in the [[Champions Guild]] for {{Coins|64,000}}. *1/21 drop from [[magpie implings]], requires {{SCP|Hunter|65|link=yes}}. *Guaranteed drop from [[Rune dragons]], requires completion of [[Dragon Slayer II]]. |leagueRegion = Unlocking {{LeagueRegion|Tirannwn}} {{okay}} will auto-complete the quest }} ==The escape of Elena== {{Needed|[[Steel full helm]], [[steel platebody]], [[steel platelegs]], [[red dye]], [[purple dye]] and one piece of [[silk]]. You can grab your [[mourner gear]] and a [[tinderbox]] to save time.|recommended=[[Stamina potion]], [[Weight-reducing clothing]], [[Ardougne teleport]], [[West ardougne teleport]]}} [[File:Edmond chathead.png|left]] To begin the quest talk to [[Edmond]], who can be found in the garden at his home in [[East Ardougne]] north of the [[Flying Horse Inn]]. (You can pick up a cabbage and spade in Edmond's garden if needed). He will tell you that [[Elena]] has been going around telling people that the plague isn't real, and has been taken by the Knights of Ardougne who claimed she was inciting rebellion against the king. Agree to help, and seek out [[King Lathas]] upstairs in his castle just south. Speak to King Lathas while not wearing Mourner gear. He will tell you that Elena was imprisoned due to spreading news that the plague is fake and as he has explained before, the façade is a necessary evil and he simply can't risk Elena exposing the truth and risking everything, including the looming threat that is the [[Dark Lord]], and thus must keep her imprisoned. Return to Edmond. The player tells him about everything they've done since the events of the [[Plague City]] quest, summing up the entire questline. He will then tell you that Elena is imprisoned in the city prison beneath the castle, and asks you to go undercover as a [[Knight of Ardougne]] and find a way to free her. You will need a [[steel full helm]], [[steel platebody]], [[steel platelegs]], [[red dye]], [[purple dye]], and one piece of [[silk]]. Use the red dye on the steel full helm to obtain an [[Ardougne knight helm]], and then talk to his wife, [[Alrena]]. You will receive a [[Ardougne knight tabard|tabard]] from her; use it on the steel platebody to obtain an [[Ardougne knight platebody]]. Equip your helm, platebody and steel platelegs. Pures who do not have 5 Defence and are unable to equip [[steel platelegs]] must talk to Edmond to polish the steel platelegs into [[Ardougne knight platelegs]]. Talk to Edmond again. He will tell you to go to the prison, which is located beneath the castle. [[File:Song of the Elves - talking to Elena.png|thumb|250px|Speaking to Elena in the city prison.]] Go to the basement of the [[Ardougne Castle]]. Here, a guard will stop you. You will tell him your name is Tim, Sue, or Sam, and be allowed to continue. Talk to [[Elena]] in the prison; she will tell you she wants to escape through a grate into the [[Ardougne Sewers]], and needs you to fetch her some [[Clear liquid|sulphuric acid]] to [[Broken Grate|dissolve]] the grate, which you can find in a [[cabinet (Song of the Elves)|cabinet]] in her home. Go to her house, which is found east of Edmond's house directly next to the log balance. Search the cabinet and select the '''thick clear liquid''', and you'll receive a vial of [[clear liquid]]. After you bring the sulphuric acid to Elena, go back and talk to Edmond while she escapes. He will tell you she already came by and is hiding to avoid being recaptured, but tells you that she is hiding in the [[The Rehnison household|Rehnison household]] in [[West Ardougne]]. The Rehnisons' house is located directly west of the [[Mourner's Headquarters]]. Talk to [[Ted Rehnison]], or his wife [[Martha Rehnison|Martha]], who will tell you the resistance is waiting in the basement below. Search the eastern bed and climb down. [[File:Song of the Elves - the Ardougne Resistance.png|thumb|250px|The Ardougne Resistance.]] Elena will tell you about the Ardougne Resistance, which she founded after discovering the plague is a hoax in order to overthrow King Lathas, and you will tell her about what you've been up to while infiltrating the mourners. [[Councillor Halgrive]] will then speak to you. He will tell you that although the council serves the king and can't overrule his decisions, they can step in as interim ruler should the king be deemed unfit to rule. Councillor Halgrive tells you of his plan to have you (disguised as a Knight of Ardougne) upset the people of Ardougne in order to make it appear as if you're doing so under the king's orders. ==Causing an insurrection== {{Needed|[[Ardougne knight armour]], [[mourner gear]], [[tinderbox]]|recommended=[[Stamina potion]], [[prayer potion]], [[armour]], [[weapon]], [[food]]}} You will need to go around [[West Ardougne|West]] and [[East Ardougne]] doing certain actions to rile up the population. Each action will convert members to the Ardougne Resistance. Keep in mind you do not have to talk to every person on this list; you just need to convert a total of 80 members. In '''West Ardougne''', dress up as a [[mourner gear|mourner]] and do the following to make the population angry: [[File:Song of the Elves - setting food on fire.png|thumb|250px|Setting food on fire to generate discord among the people.]] {{Checklist| *Talk to the [[Priest (West Ardougne)|priest]] and ask for taxes (+6) *Set the grain sacks on fire inside the [[West Ardougne#church|church]] (+8) *Tell [[Nurse Sarah]] in the south-west corner of the city behind the church about retraining (south-west of Church) (+6) *Set the grain sacks on fire outside the [[West Ardougne General Store|general store]] (+8) *Talk to [[Chadwell]] in the general store and tell him about new taxes (+4) *Set the grain sacks on fire in the central Civic Office, next to the [[clerk]] (+8) }} In '''East Ardougne''', dress up as an [[Knight of Ardougne#Trivia|Ardougne Knight]] and do the following: [[File:Song of the Elves - announcing taxes.png|thumb|250px|Announcing taxes to a silver merchant.]] {{Checklist| *Talk to all the people on the market square, '''including the town crier''', and tell them about new taxes; **[[Silk merchant]] (+2) **West [[Baker]] (+4) **East [[Baker]] (+4) **[[Silver merchant]] (+2) **[[Gem merchant]] (+2) **[[Fur trader]] (+2) **[[Spice seller]] (+2) **[[Town Crier]] (+8) *Talk to [[Zenesha]] (the armour store owner south of the square) about the new taxes (+3) *Talk to the [[estate agent]] and tell him about the new taxes (+2) *Talk to [[Probita]] (Pet Store) about taxes (+2) *Talk to [[Kortan]] or [[Aemad]] in the general store about taxes (+4) *Talk to the [[Priest (East Ardougne)|priest]] in the church about the new taxes (+6) *Talk to [[Doctor Orbon]] in the church about retraining (+6) *Talk to [[Richard (Ardougne)|Richard]] at the farm north of Ardougne about the new taxes (+4) *Set the grain sacks on fire outside the farm north of Ardougne (+4) *Talk to the [[Bartender (Flying Horse Inn)|Bartender]] at the [[Flying Horse Inn]], just north of the castle about the new taxes (+6) *Set the fish barrels on fire at the Ardougne docks (+4)}} After you have recruited 80 new members, bank to prepare yourself for the first fight of the quest. {{Instance}} Once ready for the fight, return to the [[Rehnison Household]] in West Ardougne. Enter the basement under the bed and talk to [[Elena]] to begin the battle of West Ardougne. [[File:Song of the Elves - Battle of West Ardougne.png|thumb|250px|The resistance fight the mourners of West Ardougne.]] The fight has three parts. Each part has a kill counter in the chatbox. The player will enter a battlefield in which the resistance members are engaged in combat with the mourners. The resistance fighters include generic civilians and others that you've met during the quest series. The named NPCs will never die in battle against their target, as they will eat food when their health is low. It is not required to save the resistance fighters, and the enemies will focus on you when they are attacked, even if engaged with a resistance fighter. [[Protect from Melee]] will protect you from all damage, but high-levelled players will find that decent armour will protect them from most damage. Alternatively, [[Guthan the Infested's equipment]] can be used, needing no [[food]] or [[prayer]] to complete. Mages can also use the cornering trick to stack the mobs and burst or barrage them for faster kills. Furthermore, despite some of the mourners wielding crystal halberds, they have an attack range of 1, so they can be safely trapped on corners and safespotted. In the first part, you have to kill 10 [[Mourner (Song of the Elves)|Mourners]]: *Level 24 (3) *Level 51 (5) *Level 106 (2) In the second part, head downstairs via the trapdoor and kill 11 Mourners: *Level 24 (3) *Level 51 (4) *Level 106 (4) Note: if you leave the battle having reached the second part, when you return you will be downstairs and not have to kill the initial 10 Mourners. After the second part, the fight pauses and you can bank and resupply if necessary. Talk to Elena or [[Councillor Halgrive]] to commence the battle of East Ardougne. Some of the NPCs are on the {{FloorNumber|uk=1}}. *[[Knight of Ardougne (Song of the Elves)|Knight of Ardougne]] (level 27) (5) *[[Paladin (Song of the Elves)|Paladin]] (level 49) (6) *Knight of Ardougne (level 53) (2) *Paladin (level 121) (2) Upon starting this battle, the player will be confronted by two level 121 paladins, while the resistance fighters battle the others. The resistance fighters are the same as the first battle and it is not necessary to save all of them, though the named ones will not die. After you've killed all the guards, you will see a cut-scene, where the resistance fighters bring King Lathas out, who they find hidden in a wardrobe. Players can speak to the king and ask him the reasons for his actions over the course of the quest series. In the end, he is sentenced to death by the citizens of Ardougne and executed by the [[Executioner (Song of the Elves)|executioner]]. As you and Elena watch, two hooded figures sneak up behind the two of you from the alleyway and knock both of you out with blackjacks, taking you to the [[Iorwerth Camp]]. Song of the Elves - Battle of East Ardougne.png|The resistance fight the knights and paladins of East Ardougne. Song of the Elves - the King falls.png|Lathas is captured by the resistance. Song of the Elves - the King's death.png|Lathas is sentenced to death. ==Arianwyn's plan== [[File:Song of the Elves - captured by Iorwerth.png|thumb|left|250px|The player and Elena are brought before Lord Iorwerth.]] [[File:Song of the Elves - Arianwyn rescues player.png|thumb|250px|Arianwyn comes to the player's rescue.]] A long cutscene will play, where you and [[Elena]] are brought before [[Lord Iorwerth]]. Before you both are about to be taken prisoner, [[Arianwyn]] and the elf resistance will appear to rescue you. Lord Iorwerth will reveal that he lied to [[King Lathas]] in order to access [[West Ardougne]] and reach the [[Temple of Light]] to summon [[Seren]]. Arianwyn refutes this, believing Seren to be dead; Lord Iorwerth thought the same, until he managed to break one of the seals in the [[Grand Library]], where he discovered a part of Seren remains trapped within the Temple. He will let the rebels go, believing they could be of use in their plans while they are none the wiser. Returning to [[Lletya]], Arianwyn calls a meeting, and asks you to notify [[Ysgawyn]] and Elena. You must talk to Ysgawyn first before Elena will appear. Ysgawyn can be found on the upper platform of Lletya; speak to him first. After you have done this Elena can be found in the south-west corner by the pond. After talking to Elena, return to Arianwyn and talk to him to begin the meeting. The meeting will tell of the history of the elves from the end of the [[God Wars]] to the ongoing civil war, where the city of [[Prifddinas]] was reverted to a crystal seed, and the Grand Library along with it, which Lord Iorwerth claimed he accessed. It is decided that the player and Arianwyn will investigate the [[Waterfall Dungeon]] to see whether [[King Baxtorian]] left anything behind that would explain more about the fragment of Seren. Be sure to speak to Arianwyn after the meeting, or he will not appear at [[Baxtorian Falls]]. ==Visiting Baxtorian Falls== {{Needed|[[Nature rune]], [[rope]], [[flowers]] from [[mithril|mithril seeds]] or [[adamant seeds]] or [[irit]] leaf, [[cabbage]], [[Wine of Zamorak]] or [[Zamorak brew|Zamorak brew(4)]], [[adamant chainbody]], [[black knife]] or [[black dagger]]|recommended=[[Games necklace]], [[teleport crystal]] (to get back to [[Lletya]])}} Travel to the [[Waterfall Dungeon]] (requires a [[rope]]). Go to the eastern room with the [[skeletons]] and [[skeleton mage]]s and search the northern stacked crate for [[A key (Waterfall Dungeon)|a key]], and then go to the western room with [[fire giant]]s and past the two single doors. Talk to [[Arianwyn]], and he will hand you a book, [[Ode to eternity]], with a riddle, that requires you to use items on the pillars around the tomb. '''{{Colour|red|Warning:}} If you place the items on the incorrect pillars, you will have to buy/collect the items again, and each time you enter the room, the order of numbers will change! If you leave the room before finishing the puzzle, you may be able to continue it or replace the item on the pillar you did incorrectly.''' [[File:Song of the Elves - Baxtorian's riddle pillar labelled.png|thumb|250px|Labelled pillars.]] [[File:Song of the Elves - Baxtorian lives.png|thumb|250px|Baxtorian is freed from stone.]] Each of the six items has a number as follows: # [[Nature rune]] # [[Flowers]] from [[mithril seeds]] or [[adamant seeds]], or an [[irit leaf]] # [[Black knife]] or [[black dagger]] # [[Wine of Zamorak]] or [[Zamorak brew|Zamorak brew(4)]] # [[Adamant chainbody]] # [[Cabbage]] The layout is random, and you will need to inspect the pillars to figure out the order. '''Make sure to read the book before placing any items'''. *The south-west pillar will say which number it is. Place the item corresponding to this number on the south-west pillar. *The west pillar will say it is not opposite of a number. Place the item corresponding to this number on the south-east pillar. *The north-west pillar will say the number it is next to. This number corresponds to the item to place on the west pillar. *The north-east pillar will say the number it is opposite to. This number corresponds to the item to place on the north-west pillar. *The east pillar will say what number it is not next to. This number corresponds to the item to place on the east pillar. *The south-east pillar will say what number '''isn't''' going on the pillar. This number corresponds to the item to place on the north-east pillar. After placing all of the items in the right locations, the statue of Baxtorian will spring to life, as [[Baxtorian]] himself. Arianwyn will teleport Baxtorian back to [[Lletya]]. Bank to gather items for the next section and then teleport back to Lletya yourself. ==Preparing to enter Prifddinas== {{Needed|[[Cadantine seed]], [[seed dibber]] (if the player has not learned bare-handed planting from [[Barbarian Training]]), [[runite bar]], [[hammer]], [[pickaxe]], [[pestle and mortar]], [[vial of water]]|recommended=[[Antipoison]], [[teleport crystal]] (only if searching for Lord Amlodd before Lady Trahaearn)}} Back in [[Lletya]], talk to [[Baxtorian]] and [[Arianwyn]]. Baxtorian says there may be a way to enter the [[Grand Library]] of [[Prifddinas]], to learn more about what [[Lord Iorwerth]] was planning, via the use of a specially made potion and bypassing the city walls, which have been enchanted so that only Iorwerth elves and their allies can enter; Baxtorian believes [[Lord Amlodd]], an expert in magic, would be able to break the spell. You will be asked to create the potion. Talk to [[Elena]] next to the pond, and plant a [[cadantine seed]] in the nearby farming patch, which is specially treated to yield [[elder cadantine]]. Elena will ask you to check if Arianwyn and Baxtorian have anything for you to do while you wait for the seed to grow. [[File:Isafdar map.png|alt=Isafdar map|thumb|A map of the area, showing possible routes and the locations of the traps.]] Since much of the rest of the quest takes place in the elven lands with their many traps, it is recommended to have an anti-poison potion available, since tripwires if failed will poison the player. Talk to [[Baxtorian]] and ask how to find [[Lord Amlodd]] and [[Lady Trahaearn]]. Ask how to find both elves before proceeding to save yourself time; the order in which the two are found does not matter. [[Lady Trahaearn]] gave the clue "I will journey south from the well" to [[Baxtorian]] before she left. [[Lord Amlodd]] gave [[Baxtorian]] a special [[crystal seed]] for safekeeping, which he gives to you. Talk to [[Eluned]] (found in south-west [[Lletya]], north of the patch where you planted the [[cadantine]]) to ask her to enchant the crystal seed before proceeding with the quest. '''Note: If you choose to search for Lady Trahaearn before Lord Amlodd, you will not need to bring a teleport crystal with you, since Lord Amlodd will automatically teleport you back to Lletya after speaking to him.''' ===Finding Lady Trahaearn=== [[File:Song of the Elves - waking Lady Trahaearn.png|thumb|250px|Lady Trahaearn is awakened.]] Travel west of [[Lletya]] and pass through the trees north of the [[dire wolves]]. Then run across the clearing, step over the tripwire, and head west through the 3 dense forests. Travel north through the leaf trap, and finally east to reach the cave entrance to the [[Underground Pass (dungeon)|Underground Pass]]. Inside, walk south of the [[Well of Voyage]] to inspect the blocked crevice. Afterwards, use your [[pickaxe]] on the blocked crevice to clear it and go inside. Upon entering, you'll find an Elderly Elf wearing a damaged exoskeleton. Use a [[runite bar]] and [[hammer]] on her to fix the exoskeleton. She will then be revealed to be Lady Trahaearn, and will teleport back to Lletya. Now, you should rub the crystal seed you received from Baxtorian and had enchanted by Eluned to find Lord Amlodd. However, if you chose to look for Lord Amlodd first, then return to Lletya, either on foot or with a [[teleport crystal]] (if you brought one), to make the potion. ===Finding Lord Amlodd=== After rubbing the crystal seed (make sure you had it enchanted first), you will be teleported to the [[Enchanted Valley]] where you find a [[Lord Amlodd#Mysterious Figure|Mysterious Figure]], who will ask for your opinion on the state of the valley. Choose any of the available options and the Mysterious Figure will reveal that he is Lord Amlodd. He will then teleport you back to Lletya. ===Making the potion=== Once Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn have been found, talk to [[Elena]] again and get the [[elder cadantine]]. Talk to [[Arianwyn]] to get the [[Crystal (Song of the Elves)|old crystal]], and use it with a [[pestle and mortar]] to make [[Crystal dust (Song of the Elves)|crystal dust]]. Use the elder cadantine on a [[vial of water]], and then use the crystal dust with the unfinished potion to make the [[inversion potion]]. Talk to Arianwyn and give him the potion, and a meeting will begin in which it is decided who enters the Grand Library. The group will decide on sending you in, while [[Ysgawyn]] will disagree but is overruled. Talk to [[Lord Amlodd]] (located north from Arianwyn) once more to teleport to the [[Grand Library]]. ==The Grand Library of Prifddinas== {{Needed|Six free inventory spaces|recommended=Stamina potions, food for players with low hitpoints}} Before you enter the library, take note that any unlocked memories can teleport you to other unlocked seal locations if you ask it where else you can be teleported to. You must have already heard the memory in order to teleport to it, simply solving the light puzzle doesn't enable the teleport. This can help with saving some run energy as you travel around the library. Regardless, it is recommended to bring a lot of stamina potions as the library is incredibly huge and requires a lot of running. [[Summer pie]]s are helpful for this section as they restore some run energy and health. Two sections of the library require the player navigate a gap by holding onto books, similar to the notorious wall supports in [[Mourning's End Part II]]; failing this will deal light damage to the player. {{:Song of the Elves/Warped Library guide}} ==Finding Lord Crwys and Lady Hefin== {{Needed|An [[axe]]|recommended=[[Iorwerth camp teleport]], [[antipoison]], [[teleport crystal]], [[weight-reducing clothing]].}} {{Map|height=200|width=200|mtype=pin|2176,3211|align=right|caption=The location of Lord Crwys.}} Return to [[Arianwyn]] in [[Lletya]], who will call everyone to a meeting to hear your discoveries in the [[Grand Library]]. Everyone will be shocked by the revelations, except for [[Ysgawyn]], who accuses you of lying and demands you to be taken away in chains. However, [[Baxtorian]] believes you and lets you continue reporting your discoveries. With Arianwyn still in shock, he will ask Baxtorian to temporarily lead. Ysgawyn will search for the [[Orb of light (Song of the Elves)|orb of light]] on [[Gorlah]]. In the meanwhile, you will be told to search for [[Lord Crwys]] and [[Lady Hefin]]. As before, ask Baxtorian for the clue on how to find each of them. [[File:Song of the Elves - the Elf Hermit.png|thumb|left|250px|The player finds an Elf Hermit.]] ===Finding Lady Hefin=== Lady Hefin gave Baxtorian the clue "I will walk across the water to the south" before her departure. Leave the village through the trees west, and then run south past where the [[magic trees]] are located; continue further south and jump across a trail of stepping stones over the tar lake until you reach a small island with an NPC and tent. Talk to the [[Elf Hermit]], who is actually Lady Hefin. After a short conversation, Lady Hefin teleports away. ===Finding Lord Crwys=== Lord Crwys gave Baxtorian the clue "The trees of my home will call to me" before his departure. Baxtorian also says, "The Crwys clan is a clan of woodcutters and farmers. The entire clan is close to nature, but none more than Lord Crwys." Chopping down trees in Isafdar will give a chat message that says, "The tree falls to the [compass direction]." Following these messages will lead you south of the [[Iorwerth Camp]] (refer to image above), on the western side of [[Isafdar]]. Make your way to the Elf Camp - using an [[Iorwerth camp teleport]] scroll is recommended. Note that the Iorwerth elves will attack you on sight. Cross the log balance and head south-west. You will pass a stick trap to enter the clearing, and then chop the dead tree north-west of the lake (or west of the trap) to free him. '''Note:''' You will not be able to cut the tree if you do not ask Baxtorian where to find him. Now, return back to Lletya and speak to Baxtorian, and after a short cut scene and dialogue, the other NPC's will leave. '''Note:''' You will not be able to enter the cave in the next part if you do not talk to Baxtorian. ==Recovering the orb== {{Needed|[[Armour]] and [[weapons]]|recommended=[[Food]] and [[prayer potions]] (if low level), charged [[teleport crystal]] to return quickly, [[Iorwerth camp teleport]]}} {{Instance}} [[File:Song of the Elves - fight for the orb of light.png|thumb|250px|Fighting the Iorwerth Warrior and Archer]] Head to the [[Iorwerth Camp]]. Be wary of the elves at the camp, as they'll attack you on sight, and enter the [[Iorwerth Camp cave|cave]] at the north end of the camp. Once inside, after a short dialogue, kill the [[Iorwerth Warrior]] and [[Iorwerth Archer|Archer]]. If possible, trap the warrior behind the archer; if that is not possible, then use [[Protect from Melee]] and kill the warrior first before focusing on the ranger. Alternatively, if you are confident in identifying when they are attacking, alternating between Protect from Melee and Protect from Missiles as each attacks will prevent all damage. After killing the elves, claim the [[Orb of light (Song of the Elves)|orb of light]] from the [[Chest (Song of the Elves)|chest]] and return back to [[Lletya]]; then, speak with [[Baxtorian]]. ==Tracking down Ysgawyn== {{Needed|recommended=[[Food]] and [[Antipoison]], gear and items for The attack on Lletya}} '''Note:''' It is recommended to charge your [[teleport crystal|teleport crystals]] now if you're running low, since the area becomes [[instanced]] later on and you cannot do this until much later. [[Baxtorian]] asks you in private to find [[Ysgawyn]], who has been acting unusually suspicious. He tells you [[Elena]], who is by the pond and patch where you planted the [[cadantine seed]] earlier, was the last person who saw him, and advises you to speak to her. During the conversation, select the second option, and she will tell you that she doesn't know where he went, but tells you that might be possible to search for tracks by the entrance to [[Lletya]]. It's strongly recommended to bring some food and Anti-poison for the next part as you will be avoiding the traps through the forest. [[File:Song of the Elves - Ysgawyn is accused.png|thumb|250px|The player accuses Ysgawyn of betraying the rebel elves.]] Head west out the village entrance, through the "pass tree" and inspect the [[rock (Song of the Elves)|mossy rock]] or [[mushroom (Song of the Elves)|mushroom]] (be careful of level-88 [[dire wolves]]). This will start the tracking sequence, similar to hunting [[kebbit]]s. # Continue following the tracks and inspect the rocks just before the trip wire for the second set of tracks. # Hop over the trip wire and inspect the southern inspect rock option for the third set of tracks leading across the leaves trap. # Head south and inspect the [[elven lamp (Song of the Elves)|elven lamp]] for the fourth set of tracks. # Follow the tracks south-west and inspect the mushrooms north of the large rock. # Follow the path and inspect the small rock north-east of where the tracks end for the fifth final set of tracks leading over the trip wire, where you will see Iestin and Ysgawyn. # Click on either NPC to continue the dialogue. ('''Note:''' If you do not have an inventory of very good food, such as [[sharks]] or higher, return back to a [[bank]], as you will need it, and some [[prayer potions]] if low level.) You will witness a conversation with Ysgawyn, and accuse him of being a traitor. However, he will angrily tell you that he is speaking to his informant within the [[Iorwerth clan]], [[Iestin Edern]], who provided Ysgawyn with the information about the [[Orb of light (Song of the Elves)|orb of light]], among other things. This was kept a secret from the other elves by Iestin's request, who feared that the other rebels may not be trusting of him; you will have two options to select before continuing the dialogue, before you are teleported into a cutscene, which will continue below. Iestin will then inform both of you that earlier in the day, [[Lord Iorwerth]] has discovered Lletya via a traitor among the rebel elves, and told him how to find the village, and that [[Essyllt]] is preparing their forces as they speak. The three of you will quickly rush back to town. ==The attack on Lletya== {{Needed|Combat equipment|recommended=[[Food]] and [[prayer potion]] (if low level), emergency teleport, charged [[teleport crystal]] to return quickly}} {{Instance|irs=Rebel Scout|cost=80000}} [[File:Song of the Elves - attack on Lletya.png|thumb|250px|Lletya is under attack!]] [[Lord Iorwerth]] has invaded the town of [[Lletya]] and is wreaking havoc. After a brief cutscene with [[Ysgawyn]], [[Iestin]] and [[Elena]], you are tasked with saving two children from one of the burning buildings. Head south into the building and search the various objects on the {{FloorNumber|uk=0}} and {{FloorNumber|uk=1}} (locations vary from player to player) to find the two missing children (tables, sacks, benches, cooking pots etc.). The large flames will restrict locations you are not supposed to go through. '''Note:''' There is an escape barricade located west of the instance if you wish to restock food, as you will not be able to do this before the upcoming boss fight. After saving the kids, a cut-scene will follow where Lord Iorwerth reveals that Arianwyn was the traitor, who turned once he discovered they truly wish to return [[Seren]], and forces [[Baxtorian]] to hand over the [[Orb of light (Song of the Elves)|Orb of Light]] in order to save one of the children. Ysgawyn will attempt to attack Iorwerth, but he fails to deal any damage and Essyllt will instantly kill him in retaliation. After a short dialogue and cut-scene, you will now have to fight [[Arianwyn]] in order to go after the orb once again. The moment you enter the [[instance]], use [[Protect from Missiles]] prayer. Hit-and-run tactics work best with attacking Arianwyn with [[melee gear]]. Observe the colour of his arrows. His arrows consistently hit in the 30s.[[File:Song of the Elves - fighting Arianwyn.png|thumb|250px|Fighting the traitor Arianwyn.]] Arianwyn cycles through '''three''' ranged attacks: *A green arrow is a regular attack which can be negated with Protect from Missiles. *The orange arrow is a high damage explosive attack, but it is slow moving and can be avoided by moving one step away. *The purple arrow disables active prayers. When Arianwyn reaches critical health, he will fire an orange arrow at you, knocking you out as he proceeds to escape. You will watch another cut-scene in which the surviving elves hold a funeral for Ysgawyn, and decide on what to do next. [[Islwyn]] will then ask Baxtorian about what Lord Iorwerth tried telling the rebel elves, and reveals the truth - when Baxtorian discovered where his wife was, he sent what little remained of his army to rescue her, knowing it was a trap but sending them to their deaths anyway due to his desperation to see his wife once more. Enraged, Islwyn leaves for the [[Underground Pass (dungeon)|Underground Pass]], intending to fight the [[Iorwerth Clan|Iorwerth]] on his own. During the conversation, Baxtorian will reveal the two clues when you select the options for the last two elders - [[Lady Ithell]] and [[Lady Meilyr]]- and gives you a [[Clue scroll (Song of the Elves)|clue scroll]]; head to the bank to restock on the items required for the next part. ==Finding Lady Ithell and Lady Meilyr== {{Needed|[[Spade]], 8 [[limestone brick]]s, [[saw]], [[hammer]]|recommended=[[Anti-poison]], [[stamina potions]], [[fairy ring]] access, charged [[teleport crystal]] to [[Lletya]], [[Iorwerth camp teleport]], or [[Catherby teleport]] and 800–3,200 coins for a [[charter ship]], [[Zul-Andra teleport]]}} ===Lady Ithell=== Ask how to find [[Lady Ithell]]. She gave the clue "I'll put my soul into my greatest work" before her death. You must walk around [[Lletya]] and locate the '''five''' [[symbol (Song of the Elves)|symbols]] on the walls of the buildings. The symbols are white and glowing, and easy to spot. They are located both upstairs and downstairs: [[File:Song of the Elves - Ithell symbols.png|thumb|250px|The symbols found around the village.]] *The '''first''' symbol is up the northern ladder directly above where the group meetings have been taking place. Enter the room with the [[prayer altar]]. The symbol is on the wall as you enter the room on the left of the doorway. *Leaving that symbol, head north around the upper level to the ladder in the room (upper level of the general store). The '''second''' symbol is on the northern wall between two chairs. *Head down the ladder behind you into the [[Lletya General Store|general store]]. The '''third''' symbol is on the wall to the eastern side of the shop, south of the table and south-west of the exit. *Leave the general store and head south through the ruined town to the archery shop. The '''fourth''' symbol is on the wall in the corner north of the table. *The final '''fifth''' symbol is inside the bank in the east tucked away just to the right behind the bank deposit box. Once all the symbols have been touched, you will gain some of Lady Ithell's knowledge, and you must build a [[well (Lletya)#Statue|statue]] on the [[Statue Space (Song of the Elves)|statue space]] in the centre of the village (next to the water icon on the map) with a [[saw]], [[hammer]] and eight [[limestone brick]]s (Flamtaer bag does not work). Upon building the statue and a short dialogue, [[Kelyn]] will gaze at the statue and gain all of Lady Ithell's knowledge, now becoming Lady Kelyn Ithell, before teleporting away. Return back to [[Baxtorian]] and ask for clues to Lady Meilyr in order to continue with the quest (if not already done). ===Lady Meilyr=== {{Map|mtype=pin|2225,3133|caption=The location of the first coordinate clue step to find Lady Meilyr|align=right|width=250|height=250|icon=cyanPin}} Make sure you have enough inventory space to unequip all of your equipment before continuing. Ask how to find [[Lady Meilyr]]. She gave the clue "Follow the scrolls to find me" before her departure. [[Baxtorian]] will hand the player a [[Clue scroll (Song of the Elves)|clue scroll]] with a coordinate clue. It points to a location south-east of the [[Tyras Camp]], near a [[yew tree]]; the quickest way is to teleport to [[Catherby]] and use the [[trader crewmember]] on the pier to head to the [[Tyras Camp]] Port for 800–3,200 coins (depending on [[Ring of charos]] and [[Cabin Fever]]). Once there, head through the camp and head north through the dense forest, then east and south through the next set of dense forest south of the grizzly bear/cub, and hop over either trip wire trap and south to the dig spot (4 tiles south of the triple mushroom cluster). Note: if you have never done treasure trails before, you may fail to dig up the next clue scroll even if you are standing on the correct spot. If this happens try asking the [[Observatory professor]] about treasure trails to obtain a [[sextant]], [[watch]] and [[chart]]. Destroy the Clue scroll that you have, get a new one from [[Baxtorian]] and try again. The next clue step requires travelling to [[Zul-Andra]]. A Zul-Andra teleport will take you right there; another quick way to get there is using the [[fairy ring]] {{fairycode|BJS}}, although you will need 76 [[Agility]] to use the stepping stone shortcut (can be boosted with [[summer pie]]s). Otherwise, you may move onward to [[Port tyras|Port Tyras]], go through the camp and head south from the docks, or use a [[trader crewmember]] to charter a ship to the [[Tyras Camp]] Port for 800–3,200 coins (depending on [[Ring of charos]] and [[Cabin Fever]]) and head south from there. Once there, venture to the south-east. On the east side of the northernmost hut, dig two steps north of the inconspicuous hole, or the [[STASH]] unit if players have built it, for the third clue scroll. The third step is: "''DEAD ROASTING UPSHIFTS''", which is an anagram for "''PRIFDDINAS SOUTH GATES''". To progress, dig next to the southern city gate of [[Prifddinas]]; players can quickly reach it via an [[Iorwerth camp teleport]] then going east through dense forests then north towards the southern gate. Dig one square north of the north-eastern elven lamp for the last clue scroll. The final step requires you to spin outside the entrance to the [[Underground Pass (dungeon)|Underground Pass]] in eastern [[Isafdar]] with nothing equipped. Head south across the bridge and follow the route, after which, you head north across the northern leaves trap and then further east towards the cave entrance. Once players spin, Lady Ffion Meilyr will appear for a short dialogue before departing. Use your [[teleport crystal]] to head to Lletya to bank/restock. ==Sealing the Underground Pass== {{Needed|One free inventory spot|recommended=Combat equipment, [[food]], [[prayer potion]]s and at least one [[super restore]] for battle with [[Essyllt]], emergency/bank [[teleport]], [[anti-poison]], [[stamina potion]]}} {{Instance|irs=Rebel Scout|cost=80000}} [[File:Song of the Elves - the Temple blockade.png|thumb|250px|The rebel elves, ready to defend the entrance to the Temple of Light.]] :'''Note''': Upon conclusion of the following section, you will see a cutscene and then immediately fight the final boss of the quest. It is '''highly recommended''' to bring an emergency teleport to use once the multi-combat section has concluded, in order to bank and prepare for the final boss. You will not need to complete this section again. Head for the dwarven camp in the Underground Pass. The fastest way to get there is by taking the Abyss, though players doing so should be wary of the potential of player killers near the Mage of Zamorak, and their prayer will be fully drained upon entering the Abyss. Take care that if you die fighting [[Essyllt]] you cannot re-enter from the Death Altar. You will be stopped by the game informing you that your items are being held by an allied elf. To traverse from the elven village, head north to the [[Underground Pass (dungeon)|Underground Pass]]. Pass through the well, and make your way to the bottom level. You can reach the bottom level by either failing a bridge jump or reaching the 'Descend Cave' location south on the perimeter. You will reach the dwarven camp. Speak with [[Baxtorian]] (middle), then [[Elena]]. She will give you seven [[Explosive potion (Song of the Elves)|explosive potions]] (they're stackable, so you will only need one free inventory space for these). Place them inside the [[hole (Song of the Elves)|holes]] north/outside of the camp's perimeter; then, speak to Baxtorian once again. He will tell you that [[Lord Iorwerth]] wishes to meet with him, and asks that you join him. Iorwerth will demand access to the Temple of Light, wishing to restore whatever was there. Arianwyn will be surprised, stating that it wasn't what they agreed on, but continues to side with him nonetheless. With no agreement, the siege on the camp begins. Defend the camp for four minutes from several Elf Archers and Warriors, who will also try to destroy the gate defences. Failing will reset the attempt. The elf warriors will infinitely re-spawn. Even while under attack, enemies will not remain aggressive towards you, so you will have to keep attacking different enemies and draw their attention away from the barrier. It doesn't matter if any of them are killed, but the barricade has to hold for four (4) minutes in [[multi-combat]]. Since it isn't needed to actually hit the enemies to change their attention away from the barrier to the player, any weapon will do. Newly spawned enemies always attack the barrier first, so it may be useful to use a low-damage weapon. Fast-hitting weapons also are preferable, for example [[dart]]s, [[throwing knife|knives]] or the [[goblin paint cannon]]. If using ancients, casting ice and blood burst/barrage can be useful in keeping the warriors off the gate and healing from any damage incurred. [[File:Song of the Elves - fighting Essylt.png|thumb|250px|The player battles against Essyllt.]] After keeping the barrier intact, the elven warriors come storming through the defences anyway and Elena is injured by [[Essyllt]], whom you must kill. Should you die, or leave the battle, you will not have to defend the barrier again. Essyllt is level 236 and primarily attacks with [[melee]]. He also has two special attacks that he can use at random: *Essyllt may shove you backwards with his spear, stunning you briefly, and then launch a [[ranged]] attack that can deal significant damage; this can be completely blocked by switching to [[Protect from Missiles]]. *Essyllt throws a potion at you that drains all your combat stats by 5. This attack cannot be negated with [[protection prayers]], but the stat drain can be offset by drinking a dose of [[Super restore]]. You can also ignore the stat drain if you have high enough combat stats, since the drains don't stack past 5, so your stats will only be drained to max-5. '''Note:''' After the battle with Essyllt, if your prayer runs out during the following cutscene you will be removed from the cutscene and placed outside of the battle. You can go down the tunnel to the bottom level of the Underground Pass to immediately resume the cutscene. After you defeat him, a cutscene will play before the final boss fight. Lord Iorwerth manages to pass through the crevice into the [[Temple of Light]]; Arianwyn and Baxtorian follow after him. After you check that Elena is alright, you follow as well. Lord Iorwerth will then free the [[Fragment of Seren]], despite Arianwyn's protests. Filled with newfound doubt and skepticism, he shoots Lord Iorwerth, mortally wounding him. The Fragment will then drag the player to her realm, believing the player is a servant of Seren sent to dispose of her once and for all. Song of the Elves - sealed fragment.png|The elves find the Fragment of Seren, sealed. Song of the Elves - Fragment of Seren.png|Lord Iorwerth frees the [[Fragment of Seren]]. Song of the Elves - freed fragment.png|The Fragment of Seren, enraged. ==Facing the Dark Lord== {{Instance|irs=Rebel Scout|cost=80000}} ===Mechanics=== The final part of the quest is to defeat the [[Fragment of Seren]], which may attack with either a Ranged attack with multiple hitsplats, or Melee depending on the proximity of the player. It is '''highly recommended''' to avoid her melee attack, which hits 5 times in 5 ticks, dealing up to 50 damage in total. By standing at range and using [[Protect from Ranged]], her basic Ranged attack will be greatly reduced from a max hit of 12/12 to 3/3. The Fragment of Seren has very high Melee and Ranged defence, but is weak to [[Magic]]. Using Magic for this fight is '''highly recommended'''. [[Rings of recoil]] do not work, and [[Thralls]] cannot be summoned. During the fight, she will infinitely cycle between four special attacks in order, each separated by about 15 seconds and announced via some text (usually enough time for her to do 3 normal attacks). Upon reducing her health below 666 health and then below 333 health, these attacks will be strengthened. [[File:Song of the Elves - fighting Fragment of Seren.png|thumb|250px|A player fights the Fragment of Seren.]] ====Special attack 1==== The Fragment of Seren will throw a projectile, teleporting the player next to her. After a short delay, she will perform a melee attack. This is the only attack that is not strengthened in later phases. Avoid the follow up attack by simply moving several squares away from her after being teleported. ====Special attack 2==== The Fragment of Seren will split into two, spawning a clone. Attacking the correct one (the lighter coloured shadow) will cause the clone to despawn. An additional clone will be spawned after 666 hitpoints, and another after 333 hitpoints. The clones will deal additional damage to the player, so attack the correct one as soon as possible. After she reappears, ensure you continue to keep distance from her to avoid her melee attack. ====Special attack 3==== The Fragment of Seren will spawn two Crystal Whirlwinds around her, which will heal her over time for 4-8 hitpoints per beam. An additional Whirlwind will be spawned after 666 hitpoints, and another after 333 hitpoints. As each Whirlwind is destroyed in one hit, any weapon may be used to deal with them. Taking a fast attack speed weapon such as [[darts]] or the [[toxic blowpipe]] will be helpful in destroying them quickly especially if the player has a lower damage output, though using your regular weapon of choice will also be fairly effective. Beware of walking into her melee range while pathing around her to destroy them. Using [[Blood spells]] on the beams will heal around 5-6 health per attack, despite them having only 1 health. If you do not destroy the whirlwinds, they will despawn when the final special attack is initiated. ====Special attack 4==== The Fragment of Seren will throw a projectile that immobilizes and teleports the player just outside of melee range. She will then charge up an '''unblockable''' attack based on the player's maximum hitpoints, dealing up to the player's '''maximum hit points minus 5'''. This is increased to '''minus 3''' at 666 hitpoints and '''minus 1''' at 333 hitpoints. There will be a short delay after this attack before the Fragment will attack again. The strategy for this fight will revolve around this final special attack, as you will need a method to quickly heal afterwards, as well as a method of healing to or above maximum hitpoints just before it. See the strategy section below for more details. ===Strategy=== [[Saradomin brews]] combined with [[super restores]] are recommended as your main source of healing for this fight, as you will be repeatedly reduced to 1-5 hitpoints by her final special attack. Using brews saves on inventory space and allows you to heal quickly. ====Blood spells==== The lifesteal effect from [[Blood spells]] may be used in combination with brews to heal the player to full before her final special attack. {{SCP|Magic|80}} is recommended to use [[Blood Blitz]] which will make the fight significantly easier, though it is still possible to defeat her using a lower level spell. If below 80 Magic, 1-2 [[Forgotten brew|forgotten]] or [[Ancient brew|ancient]] brews may be useful to reach the requirement to use Blood Blitz, though be aware you will need to reboost after each restore potion is used. It may also be useful to bring one stack of [[purple sweets]] or [[strawberries]] with this strategy, as even if the lifesteal has healed the player to full, the player may be damaged by an attack just before she uses her final special attack. These stackable foods allow you to heal by just a few hitpoints without incurring the debuff penalty of brews. ====Phoenix necklace==== Eating [[Nightshade|belladonna]] or [[cave nightshade]], drinking a dose of [[Zamorak brew]], or dropping a vial of [[nitroglycerin]] to activate a [[phoenix necklace]] right before the Fragment attacks with her final special attack will nullify all of her damage for a few ticks. To trigger the phoenix necklace you have to use food; while frozen by the Fragment of Seren you can't lower your hp using a [[Rock cake|dwarven rock cake]], or [[Locator orb]] from Dragon Slayer II for example. Blood spells may heal too much for the phoenix necklace to activate. More information on this strategy can be found [[Fragment of Seren#Phoenix necklaces|here]]. ====Inventory==== *[[Runes]] to cast your chosen spell ([[Blood runes]] and [[Death runes]] if using [[Blood Blitz]]), a [[rune pouch]] if you have access to one to save inventory space *[[Saradomin brews]] and [[super restores]] in a 3:1 ratio *A ranged weapon switch with a quick attack for the Crystal Whirlwind special attack to stop additional healing, such as [[Dart]]s, [[Toxic blowpipe]], or [[Magic shortbow]]. [[Chinchompa]]s on the [[Chinchompa (weapon)|Medium Fuse]] attack style will keep you out of the Fragment's melee range while maintaining the same attack speed as darts or throwing knives on accurate/long range *1-2 [[Forgotten brew|forgotten]] or [[Ancient brew|ancient]] brews if boosting for [[Blood Blitz]] *1 stack of [[purple sweets]] or [[strawberries]] *An emergency teleport ====Equipment==== *[[Bloodbark armour]] if using [[Blood spells]]; otherwise the highest level mage robes you have available (i.e. [[Mystic robes|Mystics]], [[Ahrim the Blighted's equipment|Ahrim's set]], [[ancestral robes]], [[void knight equipment]]) *A weapon to autocast [[Ancient Magicks]] if using [[Blood spells]] (i.e. [[ancient staff]], [[master wand]] etc.). Otherwise, a [[Trident of the swamp]] (at least 300 charges) '''Note:''' All damage towards her is capped at 24 *Items with magic bonus (e.g. [[seers ring]], [[occult necklace]], [[imbued god capes]], [[barrows gloves]], [[book of darkness]], [[tome of fire]] etc.) *Depending on your levels and strategy, it may be necessary to also incorporate some prayer bonus (e.g. [[amulet of fury]], [[book of darkness]], [[Dagon'hai robes]], [[Elite Void Knight equipment]] or the [[ring of the gods (i)]]) *If an [[elysian spirit shield]] is accessible, it can reduce the amount of damage you take during the one shot special attack ===The fight=== # Once in the boss fight, activate [[Protect from Missiles]] and [[Mystic Might]] or [[Augury]] (for extra magic attack). If your prayer bonus and level are high, you may also use [[Rapid Heal]] and perhaps [[Steel Skin|steel skin]] too. # Prioritise dealing damage, UNTIL the Fragment summons her healing beams. Saradomin brew doses may be consumed between ranged attacks on healing beams, without impacting DPS. If doing this, ensure your magic level is high enough to resume casting your chosen Magic spell. Once you have destroyed all the healing beams (or when they despawn), you '''must''' heal yourself to full shortly afterwards. # The Fragment of Seren will now begin charging her final special attack. If you are missing just a few hitpoints from her last attack before charging, use your [[purple sweets]] or [[strawberries]] now if you brought them, otherwise sip another brew. # Once the damage is dealt, drink at least 3 sips of brew and 1 dose of super restore potion - if you drunk more brews before the attack, then use additional restores as necessary. With [[Blood spells]] you can now let the rest of your hitpoints regenerate automatically. Make sure your super restore has restored your stats by enough to use your chosen spell, and use a [[Forgotten brew|forgotten]] or [[Ancient brew|ancient]] brew after restoring your stats if necessary. #* Players with fast reflexes can use [[Redemption]] as it will always trigger with a minimum of 60 Hitpoints, then restore prayer and pray against ranged before she can attack. #Repeat steps 2-4 as the cycle repeats. If you die during this part, you can pay 80,000 coins to collect your items from the [[rebel scout]] at the southern wall on the upper floor of the [[Underground Pass (dungeon)|Underground Pass]]. Once collected, you can enter the crevice by the scout to resume the fight. '''Be sure to collect your items from the scout first!''' If you die before collecting your items, '''all your items will be permanently lost.''' You can either return to Lletya and enter the Pass from there, or bring a [[crystal trinket]] and enter the [[Temple of Light]] via the Mourner's HQ in West Ardougne. For the Temple of Light method, you can get there from West Ardougne under the Mourner's HQ or use a [[Slayer ring]] teleport to [[dark beast]]s. Once in Temple of Light, head up the stairs in front of you, on the next floor climb the Stairs to the south-west. Next, climb down the two sets of stairs to the north. After climbing down and passing through the light door, there should be a [[Blocked Tunnel (Temple of Light)#Open|tunnel]], "Enter Tunnel" on the northern wall. This entrance can only be accessed after you've encountered the boss. While you could access the tunnel via the [[Abyss]] to the [[Death Altar]], this is {{Color|red|very dangerous}} as one death to a [[Player killer]] could result in the permanent loss of your stored items. Also watch out as your prayer will be drained to 0 upon entering the Abyss. ==Prifddinas restored== After the fight, a short cutscene will be shown where [[Lord Iorwerth]] is horrified with the Fragment's defeat. He states that even though it was angry, hurt and confused, it was also innocent. He states that he hopes they're happy for what they've done to Seren before dying. Following the defeat of Lord Iorwerth and the Dark Lord, the player is brought to the gates of [[Prifddinas]]. Outside the newly regrown crystal city, [[Arianwyn]] will meet with the player. The player thanks Arianwyn for stopping Iorwerth, but Arianwyn claims that he had to, and decides to leave the elven lands for good. Ridden with guilt for hurting his fellow kind, he decides to travel the world to reflect his own actions, and hopefully redeem himself in the process. He bids farewell to the player as they make their way to the gates, where Baxtorian and Elena wait for them. Baxtorian declares that Prifddinas will be open to all, seeing how one of their own caused so much destruction, welcoming both you and Elena as the first humans to the city. '''Congratulations, quest complete!''' Song of the Elves - Fragment of Seren defeated.png|The Fragment of Seren, defeated. Song of the Elves - Arianwyn leaves.png|Arianwyn, ridden with guilt, leaves. Song of the Elves - Prifddinas restored.png|The gates of Prifddinas are opened. ==Rewards== {{Quest rewards |name = Song of the Elves |image = [[File:Song of the Elves reward scroll.png|center]] |qp = 4 |rewards = *{{SCP|Agility|40,000|link=yes}} experience *{{SCP|Construction|40,000|link=yes}} experience *{{SCP|Farming|40,000|link=yes}} experience *{{SCP|Herblore|40,000|link=yes}} experience *{{SCP|Hunter|40,000|link=yes}} experience *{{SCP|Mining|40,000|link=yes}} experience *{{SCP|Smithing|40,000|link=yes}} experience *{{SCP|Woodcutting|40,000|link=yes}} experience *Access to [[Prifddinas]] **Ability to fight [[Zalcano]] **Access to [[The Gauntlet]] **Access to [[Trahaearn Mine]] **Access to [[Iorwerth Dungeon]] **Ability to change [[respawn]] point to [[Prifddinas]] **Ability to move [[Player-owned house]] to [[Prifddinas]] **Ability to teleport to [[Prifddinas]] with a [[teleport crystal]] **Access to a new [[spirit tree]] destination in [[Prifddinas]] **Ability to make [[divine potion]]s **Ability to make [[crystal equipment|crystal tools, armour and weaponry]] Unlocks: * [[Arianwyn]] (as ''Elven Traitor'') and [[Essyllt]] accessible in the [[Nightmare Zone]]. * The ability to dock at Prifddinas and utilise its port services * The ability to use Port Tyras' notice board }} ==Transcript== {{Hastranscript|Quest}} ==Music== {| class="wikitable sortable" |- !Name ! class="unsortable" | Unlock details ! class="unsortable" | Music track |- |[[Stand Up and Be Counted]] |Unlocked during the battle of West Ardougne |[[File:Stand Up and Be Counted.ogg]] |- |[[Song of the Elves (music track)|Song of the Elves]] |Unlocked in the warped Grand Library |[[File:Song of the Elves.ogg]] |- |[[The Fires of Lletya]] |Unlocked during the Iorwerth attack on Lletya |[[File:The Fires of Lletya.ogg]] |- |[[No Pasaran]] |Unlocked during the defence of the dwarven camp in the Underground Pass |[[File:No Pasaran.ogg]] |- |[[The Fragment]] |Unlocked during the fight with the [[Fragment of Seren]] |[[File:The Fragment.ogg]] |} ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 30 November 2022 |update = The Garden of Death & More |poll = Future Quests & Combat Achievement Rewards |change = The following skill experience rewards have increased: *[[Agility]] from 20,000 to 40,000. *[[Construction]] from 20,000 to 40,000. *[[Farming]] from 20,000 to 40,000. *[[Herblore]] from 20,000 to 40,000. *[[Hunter]] from 20,000 to 40,000. *[[Mining]] from 20,000 to 40,000. *[[Smithing]] from 20,000 to 40,000. *[[Woodcutting]] from 20,000 to 40,000. }} {{Subject changes footer}} ==Trivia== [[File:Song of the Elves login screen.png|thumb|300px|The log in screen upon the release of the quest.]] *Song of the Elves is the first quest released in ''Old School'' to complete a quest line from the original game. The quest itself derives a number of elements from its equivalent ''RuneScape 3'' questline, primarily {{RSL|Plague's End}}, though there are some notable differences in the story: **Arianwyn never betrays the player; instead, he stays loyal to the rebel elves and helps defeat the Dark Lord. He is also revealed to be the current Lord Cadarn, giving his name a more proper meaning. **The Dark Lord is the result of Seren using {{RSL|Haluned|one of her avatars}} as a vessel for the part of herself obsessed with death. She banished it before shattering herself in order to protect her elves from being corrupted by it. **Seren made the elves aware of The Dark Lord's existence, rather than it being forbidden knowledge Lord Iorwerth discovers in the Grand Library. **Lord Iorwerth is killed by the Dark Lord. **The West Ardougne Revolution and the overthrowing of King Lathas is much different. Instead of the whole city going on fire, it is only a single attack on the Mourner HQ. The group the player works with is different, consisting of Elena, Ted and Martha Rehnison, Jethick, Nurse Sarah, Carla, and Koftik. In addition, you do not fight the Knights of Ardougne nor [[Hero]]es; rather, they join you against the overthrowing of the King, while the Paladins and Warrior Women fight against you. In the end, you fight Sir Hugo, who guards the King. {{Listen |title = Scape Crystal |align = right |filename = Scape Crystal.ogg |desc = Song that plays on the updates login screen. }} *You were given the choice to either send King Lathas in exile or execute him (though in the case of the former he is killed by the Dark Lord anyway). **Essyllt could be spared during the attack on Mourner HQ, potentially making him the third known Iorwerth survivor, along with Morvran and Iesten. He is also cowardly, begging for mercy upon being defeated, a stark contrast to his ''Old School'' variant who is more aggressive. The player also has significantly more interactions with him in ''RuneScape''. **Lletya's location is not revealed, sparing it from an attack by the Iorwerth elves. **[[File:Song of the Elves login screens.jpg|thumb|width=300x300|Competition finalists for the login screen that were voted on by the community.]]Another quest in ''RuneScape'', {{RSL|Within the Light}}, came chronologically in between Mourning's End Part II and Plague's End, and is omitted completely from ''Old School RuneScape''. In addition, Baxtorian's [[File:Song of the Elves Promotional Artwork.jpg|thumb|300px|A piece of official artwork of the quest, showing the player in the Grand Library.]]involvement in Song of the Elves is derived from the sequel to Plague's End, {{RSL|The Light Within}} (he is completely absent from Plague's End). [[File:Seal of the Forgotten.png|thumb|170px|The Seal of the Forgotten, representing the Cywir Clan.]] *The Seal of the Forgotten is dedicated to the former 9th [[Elven Clan]], the {{RSL|Cywir}}, who chose to remain on the Elven homeworld of Tarddiad. **The symbol used for the Cywir Clan is erroneous as the Cywir Clan has no confirmed colour or symbol. The symbol used is derived from an item called {{RSL|Seal of the Cywir}}, which is named because they are dropped by the Seren faction general and clan leader of the Cywir Clan, {{RSL|Helwyr}}. The other factions led by other generals have the exact same symbol, which is a motif taken from the {{RSL|Heart of Gielinor}}, albeit in different colours corresponding to their respective faction. Purple for [[Zaros]], red for [[Zamorak]], green for [[Sliske]] and the cyan colour representing [[Seren]] rather than the Cywir Clan itself. *When you speak to Alrena with silk and purple dye already in your inventory, she comments about how lucky it is that the player already has the necessary items. *When the player speaks with the Knight of Ardougne, they remark on how they are constantly attacked and pickpocketed, the latter referring to how players use them for [[Thieving]] training. *If the player attempts to tax the Bank of Ardougne by talking to any banker, they reply that in return the player would have to be taxed for their bank usage as well. The player immediately rejects this idea and responds with "On second thought... forget I said anything." The same joke is made when attempting to collect taxes from bankers on behalf of [[King Gjuki Sorvott IV]] during [[The Fremennik Isles]]. *If the player has an Elf Slayer task, none of the enemies that you need to kill for this quest count towards it. *When you speak to [[Elena]] about growing the elder cadantine, she asks you to fetch a cadantine seed and a seed dibber while she prepares the patch in the meantime. If you already have these items in your inventory and state you already have them, she replies with "Well it also happens that I've actually already prepared the patch". Which could be a joke to players reading ahead on a guide to know which items they'd have to bring. *Upon examining one of the dead elves, the text will read "At least they didn't take an arrow to the knee.", which is a reference to a quote from the game {{wp|The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim}}. *Dialogue with [[Lord Iorwerth]] throughout the quest makes reference to the {{wp|List of Star Wars films|Star Wars films}}, most notably {{wp|Star Wars: Episode II – Attack of the Clones|Attack of the Clones}}, with Lord Iorwerth quoting Sith lords. Examples include "they may still be of use to us," (which Darth Vader says in A New Hope), and "brave of you [to come here Arianwyn], but foolish," (which Count Dooku says to both Anakin Skywalker and Mace Windu in Attack of the Clones). Responses by characters also match dialogue from the Star Wars prequel, such as when Islwyn says they won't barter, and when Iorwerth tells Arianwyn that he once thought as he did. *A week before the release of the quest, it was added to the [[Quest List]] interface with the text in grey ({{colour|#9f9f9f|Song of the Elves}}). When the quest was clicked on, the usual interface opened but with the description: {{Cquote2|{{colour|#000080|COMING SOON to}} {{colour|#800000|Old School RuneScape}}{{colour|#000080|...}} {{colour|#000080|I can start this quest}} {{colour|#800000|after the next update}} {{colour|#000080|by talking to}} {{colour|#800000|Edmond}} {{colour|#000080|at his home, north of the}} {{colour|#800000|Flying Horse Inn}} {{colour|#000080|in}} {{colour|#800000|East Ardougne}}{{colour|#000080|.}}}} *If you talk to Elena before the attack on the Underground Pass, she will tell the player not to die and will say they care for each other. *The track that plays during the attack on the Underground Pass is called "[[No Pasaran]]," Spanish for "They will not pass," a saying used by the Republican faction of the Spanish Civil War in their fight against the Nationalists, which in subsequent years became a common {{wp|They shall not pass|slogan of anti-fascist resistance}}. *The player is asked their name multiple times during the quest, to which their response depends on the player's selected [[pronouns]], with "Tim?" if he/him, "Sue?" if she/her, or "Sam?" if they/them. This is a possible reference to Tim the Enchanter from ''{{wp|Monty Python and the Holy Grail}}'', who answers King Arthur in a similar manner. {{Song of the Elves}}
